key_insight: A reconstructed discontinuous Galerkin method on unfitted meshes achieves stable high-order interface discretization while enabling a low-order preconditioner with mesh-, coefficient-, and interface-independent conditioning.
engineering_relevance: This offers a robust finite-element strategy for elliptic interface problems where geometry cuts the mesh, improving solver scalability and reliability in multiphysics and material-interface simulations.
method: Build a high-order reconstructed discontinuous space from local least-squares fitting with added constraints.
Enforce interface jump conditions weakly using Nitsche’s penalty formulation in a cut DG framework.
Derive norm equivalence to a piecewise-constant space and use it to form an optimal preconditioner with multigrid inversion for the lowest-order system.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments in 2D and 3D report optimal convergence rates and show that the preconditioned condition number remains robust with respect to mesh size, coefficient contrast, and interface position.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and stability of the local constrained reconstruction near complex interfaces, so implementation details of the fitting and cut-cell handling matter.
paper_title: Preconditioned Discontinuous Galerkin Method for Elliptic Interface Problem on Unfitted Mesh with Reconstructed Discontinuous Approximation
